introduction:
In Karnataka, the Caste / Caste Income Certificate is issued by the Tahsildar (Revenue Department) for SC, ST, OBC (Category I, IIA, IIB, IIIA, IIIB), and EWS applicants. It is mandatory for availing reservations in government education, jobs, and welfare schemes.

Applications can be submitted online through the Nadakacheri / AJSK Portal (Anubhava Janaseva Kendra) or offline at the nearest Nadakacheri / Tahsildar office.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Issued by the Tahsildar of the applicant's native taluk
  • Processing time: 7–15 working days
  • Fee: ₹15–₹25 (via Nadakacheri)
  • SC/ST certificates are permanent; OBC certificates are valid for 3 years
  • Online service available at Nadakacheri Portal (ajsk.in)
  • Aadhaar-linked application mandatory for online submissions
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Identity Proof
  • Aadhaar Card (mandatory)
  • Voter ID
  • Ration Card
  • Passport
Caste Proof
  • Father's Caste Certificate
  • School Transfer / Study Certificate mentioning caste
  • RTC (Revenue Records) of native village
  • Old Ration Card mentioning caste
Additional
  • Income Certificate (for OBC)
  • Residence / Domicile Certificate
  • Passport-size photographs (2)
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Nadakacheri / AJSK Portal

Use the Nadakacheri Portal to apply from home.

  1. Visit Portal
    Go to nadakacheri.karnataka.gov.in
  2. Login / Register
    Register using your mobile number. OTP verification is mandatory.
  3. Select Service
    Choose 'Caste Certificate' from the list of Revenue services.
  4. Fill Application
    Enter name, DOB, father's name, caste category, native taluk, and district.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned PDFs/JPGs of Aadhaar, caste proof, and photo.
  6. Pay Fee
    Pay ₹15–₹25 via online payment.
  7. Submit & Track
    Submit and track using your acknowledgement number at the portal.
  8. Download Certificate
    Once approved, download the digitally signed certificate from the portal.
You can also apply at any AJSK (Anubhava Janaseva Kendra) or CSC centre near you.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Nadakacheri / Tahsildar Office

  1. Visit Nadakacheri or Tahsildar Office
    Visit the Nadakacheri or Tahsildar office of your native taluk.
  2. Collect & Fill Form
    Obtain the prescribed application form and fill all required details.
  3. Attach Documents & Submit
    Submit self-attested document copies along with the filled form and pay the fee.
  4. Village Accountant Verification
    The Village Accountant (VA) will conduct a field verification of caste and residence.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect the caste certificate from the Tahsildar office after approval, typically within 7–15 working days.
Processing Time: 7–15 working days.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Application

Track at Nadakacheri Portal using your acknowledgement number.

OBC Validity

OBC certificates are valid for 3 years. Renew before expiry using the same portal.

Grievance

Call 080-2235-5000 or visit Karnataka Sakala Portal for complaints.
